scenario.context: Station 6 of the daily run: assess protection needs for workplace software and clients.
scenario.mission: You classify protection needs for software, the client, and the data flow of a workstation.

station.profile: CRM, file storage, office tools, and a standardized workstation client
constraints: Connect data value and outage impact

customerRequest.subject: Station 06: justify protection needs for the CRM workstation
customerRequest.body: Please assess the protection needs and minimum protection for CRM, file storage, and the related workstation client in a traceable way.

questions.title: Strongest driver of protection need
questions.prompt: Which factor determines the protection need most strongly?

options.text: Sensitivity of the data and impact on the business process
options.rationale: Data value and process impact are the decisive criteria.

options.text: Only the color of the case
options.rationale: Appearance has no relation to protection needs.

options.text: Only the purchase price of the client
options.rationale: Costs alone do not define protection need.

feedback.correct: Correct: data value and operational impact are decisive.
feedback.wrong: Not suitable: protection need must be tied to data and process impact.

questions.title: Assessment aspects for workplace software and clients
questions.prompt: Which aspects are relevant for the protection-needs assessment? (Multiple selections)

options.rationale: Data value is a major factor in determining protection need.

options.text: Patch and update capability of software and client
Impact in case of outage or manipulation
options.rationale: Damage impact is a core part of the assessment.

options.text: Only the personal preferences of individual users
options.rationale: Protection need is not a matter of taste.

options.text: Dependencies between software, client, and data flow
options.rationale: These interactions also have to be considered.

feedback.correct: Correct: you weigh data value, operations, and the technical basis cleanly.
feedback.wrong: Partly wrong: please connect data, outage impact, and system dependencies more strongly.

questions.title: Methodology for the CRM workstation
questions.prompt: Arrange the steps for determining protection need.
items: Classify protection need for software and client
Document minimum protection and residual items
Capture software, client, and data flow
Assess outage, manipulation, and access consequences
correctOrder: Capture software, client, and data flow
Assess outage, manipulation, and access consequences
Classify protection need for software and client
Document minimum protection and residual items

feedback.correct: Correct: this builds the protection-needs assessment in a professional and traceable way.
feedback.wrong: Not ideal: first capture and assess, then classify and document.

questions.title: Build a simple priority score
questions.prompt: A model adds data value 4, outage impact 5, and integrity risk 3. What is the total score?

questions.title: Briefly justify protection need
questions.prompt: In 4-6 sentences, justify the protection need of a CRM workstation with one core measure and one residual point.
questions.expectedOutline: State data value and outage impact, derive a core measure, and make the residual point transparent.

feedback.correct: Correct: the justification is professionally sound and useful in operations.
feedback.wrong: Please separate protection need, measure, and residual point more clearly.
